additional_rules.name: business-logic-in-view
additional_rules.message: Business logic method defined in view/viewset — move to a service module
additional_rules.fix_hint: Create a {app}/services.py file and move business logic there. The view should only call the service and serialize the result.

additional_rules.name: orm-in-view-without-service
additional_rules.message: Direct ORM query in view — use a service or model manager method instead
additional_rules.fix_hint: Move ORM queries to services.py or a custom model manager. Views should delegate data access, not perform it directly.

additional_rules.name: missing-select-related
additional_rules.message: Loop over queryset with attribute access — likely N+1 query. Use select_related() or prefetch_related().
additional_rules.fix_hint: Add .select_related('field') or .prefetch_related('field') to the queryset before the loop.

additional_rules.name: raw-sql-with-format
additional_rules.message: Raw SQL with string formatting — SQL injection risk. Use parameterized queries.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Use .raw('SELECT ... WHERE id = %s', [user_id]) or cursor.execute('... WHERE id = %s', [user_id]) with a separate params list.

additional_rules.name: mark-safe-usage
additional_rules.message: mark_safe() used — verify this is not applied to user-supplied content
additional_rules.fix_hint: Only use mark_safe() on strings generated entirely by trusted code. User input must be escaped first with django.utils.html.escape().

additional_rules.name: irreversible-migration
additional_rules.message: RunPython migration without reverse_code — migration is not reversible
additional_rules.fix_hint: Add a reverse_code argument: RunPython(forwards_func, reverse_code=backwards_func). Use RunPython.noop for intentionally irreversible migrations with explicit documentation.

additional_rules.name: print-in-production
additional_rules.message: print() in application code — use structured logging instead
additional_rules.fix_hint: Replace print() with logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) and logger.info() / logger.error().

additional_boundaries.name: Views must not import from other app models directly
